mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and residential fixed asset investment indicate a synchronized contraction in credit-fueled demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of peak industrial production, sustained month-over-month declines in the industrial production index signal supply-side bottlenecks resolving into excess capacity, which historically precedes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during late-cycle expansions do not correlate with negative 60-day forward returns (Spearman rho > -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, generating positive forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as institutional investors price in higher near-term cash flows.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.0 percent year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real purchasing power and forces monetary tightening, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI inflation above 3.0% fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances across multiple decades.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and unemployment rate stability indicates a structural labor mismatch that suppresses wage-driven consumption demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of PAYEMS and UNRATE does not produce a statistically significant negative predictive relationship for 60-day forward returns (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery regime, a sustained month-over-month increase in housing starts combined with rising industrial production signals broad-based economic acceleration, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical sectors outperform.
failure_condition: Rising housing starts and industrial production fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of housing-led recovery episodes.
